title: Chapter 6 Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of layouts and styling!

questions.question: Which widget arranges children horizontally?
questions.explanation: Row places children in a horizontal line.

questions.question: MainAxisAlignment controls alignment along the cross axis.
questions.explanation: MainAxisAlignment controls the main axis; cross axis is controlled by CrossAxisAlignment.

questions.question: The widget that forces its child to fill remaining space in a Row/Column is _____.
questions.explanation: Expanded forces the child to occupy the remaining space.

questions.question: What is the difference between Expanded and Flexible?
options: Expanded forces its child to fill space, Flexible allows the child to be smaller
Flexible can only be used in Columns
Expanded has a flex parameter, Flexible does not
questions.explanation: Expanded forces its child to take up all available space; Flexible allows the child to be smaller.

questions.question: Container can have both color and decoration simultaneously.
questions.explanation: If both are set, color is ignored. Use decoration for full styling.

questions.question: Which property of Container adds space inside the container around its child?
questions.explanation: Padding adds space inside the container.

questions.question: To add a border radius to a Container, you use a _____ in the decoration.
questions.explanation: BoxDecoration takes a borderRadius property of type BorderRadius.

questions.question: The Stack widget paints its children in order from last to first.
questions.explanation: Children are painted in order: first child at bottom, last at top.

questions.question: Which widget allows you to position a child at a specific offset within a Stack?
questions.explanation: Positioned is used inside a Stack to place a child at a specific location.

questions.question: To access the current theme, use Theme._____.
questions.explanation: Theme.of(context) returns the theme data.

questions.question: MediaQuery provides information about the screen size and orientation.
questions.explanation: MediaQuery gives access to device dimensions, text scaling, etc.

questions.question: Which widget would you use to build a layout that adapts based on the available width?
questions.explanation: LayoutBuilder provides the parent's constraints, allowing conditional layouts.

questions.question: To add equal spacing between children in a Row, set mainAxisAlignment to _____.
questions.explanation: spaceEvenly distributes space evenly including before first and after last.

questions.question: A SizedBox with no child creates an empty space of given dimensions.
questions.explanation: SizedBox can be used as a spacer.

questions.question: Which EdgeInsets constructor creates symmetric horizontal and vertical padding?
questions.explanation: EdgeInsets.symmetric allows separate horizontal and vertical values.

questions.question: A BoxShadow is defined inside a _____.
questions.explanation: BoxDecoration has a boxShadow property.

questions.question: The 'flex' parameter in Expanded determines how much space the child takes relative to other expanded children.
questions.explanation: flex is a factor used to proportionally distribute space.

questions.question: What does CrossAxisAlignment.start do in a Column?
options: Aligns children to the left (if LTR)
questions.explanation: CrossAxisAlignment in a Column aligns children horizontally (left/right).

questions.question: To make a widget take 50% of its parent's width, you can use a _____.
questions.explanation: FractionallySizedBox sizes its child to a fraction of the available space.

questions.question: The 'mainAxisSize' property can be set to MainAxisSize.min to make the Row/Column take only the space needed.
questions.explanation: MainAxisSize.min causes the widget to shrink-wrap its children.

questions.question: Which widget would you use to create a circular avatar?
questions.explanation: CircleAvatar is specialized for avatars, but ClipOval and Container with borderRadius can also create circles.

questions.question: To set a fixed width for a Container, use the _____ parameter.
questions.explanation: Container has width and height parameters.

questions.question: Theme.of(context) can be called from any widget that has a BuildContext.
questions.explanation: Yes, as long as there's a Theme ancestor.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'fit' property in BoxDecoration for an image?
options: It determines how the image should be inscribed into the box
questions.explanation: BoxFit controls how the image fills the box (cover, contain, etc.).

questions.question: To change the app's primary color, set the _____ property in ThemeData.
questions.explanation: primarySwatch is a common way to define the main theme color.
